title: Resist Suggestibility: - Slow Down: Reflect Before Accepting Others’ Suggestions as True
subtitle: Question Leading Suggestions
description:
To resist suggestibility:
- Slow down: Reflect before accepting others’ suggestions as true.
- Seek evidence: Verify claims rather than relying on someone’s framing.
- Ask critical questions: “Is this my thought, or has it been influenced?”

Example: If someone suggests a memory (“Remember when we all agreed?”), ask for details to confirm.

howTo.title: Pause for 10 seconds
howTo.summary: When someone makes a claim, take a visible pause for 10 seconds. This breaks automatic acceptance and allows you to reflect on the suggestion.

howTo.title: Ask for evidence
howTo.summary: Follow your pause with a verification question. Ask for specifics or sources to clarify the claim and ensure you are not accepting misleading information.

howTo.title: Journal your thoughts
howTo.summary: After flagged interactions, spend 30 to 90 seconds writing a brief note about the claim, who made it, and how you responded. This reinforces your memory and decision-making.

howTo.title: Track your progress
howTo.summary: Set a goal to practice these steps regularly. Count the number of times you pause and verify claims each week to measure your improvement.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results and reflections in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and reinforce the habit. Access it at https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/suggestibility-defense-trainer/
